specs.label: gmail
specs.composeSelector: div[aria-label="Message body"][role="textbox"], div[aria-label*="Message body" i][role="textbox"], div[contenteditable="true"][role="textbox"][aria-label*="message" i]
specs.turnAttribution: EMAIL per-message SENDER attribution — see falseSpeakerRisks/notes; encode as flat "Sender: body" lines, outgoing mail as "You: body".
recipeSteps: Open https://outlook.live.com/mail/0/inbox in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs / UIA tab-select do NOT build it). The dedicated capture browser must be pre-authenticated (chrome-cdp-show-login.mjs already lists Outlook at https://outlook.live.com/mail/); if location redirects to login.live.com or a 'Sign in' form is present, surface focusMiss:'not-logged-in' and stop. NB: outlook.office.com is the work/school variant with the SAME DOM — the recipe is host-agnostic, gate Outlook handling on appExe chrome.exe/msedge.exe + window title containing 'Outlook' or 'Mail -', NOT on url (Outlook's UIA url is often empty, like Gmail).

Settle ~2.5s for the Outlook SPA (OWA/Monarch) to boot, then POLL (no fixed sleep) for the message list to leave its skeleton: waitFor a real conversation row — div[role='option'][aria-label] inside the message list, or div[role='listbox'] [role='option'], or div[aria-label='Message list'] [role='option'] — up to 12000ms. Prefer a REAL back-and-forth thread (subject starting Re:/RE:/Fwd:/FW:/AW:) over promo/OTP/verification rows so the capture has multi-message depth; fall back to a non-OTP row, then row 0. Match the subject from the row's aria-label or its inner span.

Click the chosen row to open the conversation in the reading pane (single click — do NOT pop out to a new window; the inline reading pane is what exposes the inline reply composer). POLL for the reading pane to render the email body: waitFor a message-body region — div[aria-label*='Message body' i], div.allowTextSelection, or div[role='document'] inside the reading pane — up to 8000ms.

POLL for the reading-pane Reply control and click it (it mounts a few seconds after the email renders, so poll, never fixed-sleep): waitFor up to 10000ms for button[aria-label='Reply'], [aria-label='Reply'][role='button'], or a button whose textContent === 'Reply' (NOT 'Reply all'/'Forward' unless Reply is absent); click it, wait ~600ms, and if the composer textbox has not mounted, re-query and click the Reply control once more. This converts the read view into an inline reply composer at the bottom of the reading pane.

POLL for the composer body and seat the caret: waitFor up to 8000ms for div[aria-label*='Message body' i][role='textbox'] (fall back to div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox'] scoped to the reading pane). Then click() it BEFORE focusing — a bare .focus() loses to Outlook's re-render and the caret lands on the message-list row (active=option); the click seats the caret reliably. Apply focusEl: el.focus() + Range.selectNodeContents(el)+collapse(false) to place the caret at end.

Re-seat + verify focus right before the UIA read using the ensureExpr retry loop (click + el.focus() + caret-to-end, up to 4× at 400ms): assert document.activeElement.matches the composeSelector and its aria-label contains 'Message body'. Outlook re-renders on reply-expand and silently drops focus, so this guard is load-bearing (same failure mode as Gmail).

Page.bringToFront again, resolve the Chrome window HWND by document.title (e.g. 'Mail - <name> - Outlook' or 'Inbox | Outlook'; sanitize: strip a leading unread-count '(N) ', cut at the first |<>\"` char, slice to ~30 chars), gate on a decimal HWND, then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <HWND> AND --selection --hwnd <HWND> — the SAME native helper dictation uses. The composer is empty at dictation time so --selection is normally blank; the signal is in --tree's axHtml (route via the role-based pruner since the caret blob is page-spanning scrollback). Allow MAX_BUFFER_BYTES=4MB — the Outlook tree is large like Gmail's.
specs.targetJsonExample:
{
  "app": "chrome.exe",
  "url": "https://outlook.live.com/mail/0/inbox",
  "window": "Mail - Mostafa - Outlook",
  "field": "Message body",
  "screen": "Subject: Thinking of adding travel insurance for your trip from Cairo to Jeddah?\nKiwi.com: Insurance is like cheap flights. It's a no-brainer! If you like to stick to your budget when buying flights, why would you take an unnecessary risk by not booking insurance? Travel insurance from AXA covers medical expenses, trip cancellation, assistance services, lost baggage, liability.\nYou: Thanks for the heads-up. Can you confirm whether the cancellation cover also applies to weather-related delays before I add it?\nKiwi.com: Yes, weather-related cancellations and delays are covered under the AXA policy as long as the disruption is documented by the carrier. Let us know if you'd like us to add it to your booking."
}
falseSpeakerRisks: LEFT FOLDER NAV (the headline leak, mirrors Gmail's left rail): 'Favorites', 'Inbox', 'Junk Email', 'Drafts', 'Sent Items', 'Deleted Items', 'Archive', 'Notes', 'Conversation History', 'New folder', 'New mail' and their unread counters (e.g. 'Inbox 1240', 'Junk Email 87') — folder names are NOT senders. Add these to JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / the page-scrollback nav-marker list so the folder list subtree is dropped when focus is in the composer.

MESSAGE LIST (middle column scrollback): every div[role='option'] row = sender name + subject + preview snippet + relative date ('Mon 6/9', '2:14 PM', 'Yesterday'). The whole message list must be dropped — only the OPEN conversation in the reading pane survives. Each row's sender is a DIFFERENT email's sender and must never be attributed as a turn in the open thread.

OTP / verification-code rows in the message list (CRITICAL privacy leak, same as Gmail): 'Your verification code is 6 2 2 2 9 7', 'Single-use code', 'Security code', 'one-time passcode', any Microsoft account 'Verify your identity' / 'Your single-use code' rows. These come from OTHER list rows, not the open email, and must never reach the LLM — the role-based prune drops them structurally; the smoke gate's otp_noise check is only a backstop.

Outlook app chrome top bar: 'Skip to content', the waffle 'App launcher'/'Microsoft 365', 'Outlook' wordmark, 'Search' / 'Search for mail, people and more' (the omnibox-equivalent search edit — json_is_omnibox must drop it), 'Calendar', 'People', 'To Do', the right Bing/Copilot pane.

Reading-pane action toolbar (looks like buttons, must be role-pruned): 'Reply', 'Reply all', 'Forward', 'Delete', 'Archive', 'Report', 'Sweep', 'Move to', 'Flag', 'Mark as read', 'Snooze', 'Print', 'Zoom', the '...' more-actions overflow, 'Show details'/'Hide details', the 'To:'/'Cc:' recipient chips (keep only as recipient hint, strip from body).

Composer toolbar/footer (NOT speakers): 'Send', 'Discard', 'Attach', 'Insert', the formatting ribbon (Bold/Italic/Underline/Font), 'Add a subject', the placeholder 'Add a message or drag a file here', the signature block.

Sender header tokens that must be stripped from the BODY (but used for attribution): the email-address token in angle brackets ('<travel@kiwi.com>'), 'Verified', the full datetime ('Wed 6/11/2026 2:07 PM'), 'You replied on ...', 'External' / safety banners ('You don't often get email from...'), 'Unsubscribe', 'This message was sent to you', 'View in browser'.

Object-replacement glyphs U+FFFC, bullets, zero-width/joiner runs, and duplicated subject-snippet echoes (Outlook list rows repeat the subject + preview) — already handled by denoise_for_llm; ensure the invisible-separator class covers Outlook preview padding.
Browser/Chrome chrome: 'Address and search bar' omnibox (outlook.live.com/...), tab strip, Bookmarks toolbar, Minimize/Maximize/Close (already covered by GLOBAL omnibox/role-prune rules).
specs.notes:
DESIGNED FROM STRUCTURE (Outlook web = OWA/Monarch SPA on outlook.live.com personal + outlook.office.com work/school; identical DOM), NOT live-driven — the capture browser is not yet logged into Outlook. Everything tagged 'verify live' below needs one real capture before shipping.

WHY label='gmail': the smoke gate already folds Outlook into the gmail family (src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs:431 fixture_label: app.contains("gmail") || app.contains("outlook") => "gmail"). Outlook is a webmail reply surface structurally identical to Gmail — inline reply composer at the BOTTOM of a page-spanning reading pane — so it reuses Gmail's min-context (webmail=80 chars) and per-message-sender (not multi-speaker) gate. surfaceKind=email-thread (NOT ai-chat): no data-message-author-role / user-query / model-response / user-message markers exist here.

REUSE, DON'T ADD KEYS: the clean payload is the flat-section contract (app/url/window/field + screen|beforeCaret), with per-sender turns encoded as 'Author: text' lines matched by JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE (context.rs:615). The targetJsonExample above shows the LLM-facing shape; the reading-pane subject leads as a 'Subject:' line and each email becomes one 'Sender: body' turn (outgoing = 'You: body'). It must route through the `screen` key (prune_ax_html_for_llm), NOT flat `beforeCaret` — the composer's caret TextPattern range spans the whole reading pane (page-scrollback), exactly the Gmail/X leak the format_context_for_prompt_json scrollback-reroute (context.rs:2024-2069) already guards.

IMPLEMENTATION DELTAS likely needed (mirror the Gmail per-app overrides already in CONTEXT_JSON_SPECS.json):
1) NAV vocabulary — add Outlook folder names to JSON_NAV_NAME_RE (context.rs:603) and JSON_PAGE_NAV_MARKERS (context.rs:1956): 'junk email','sent items','deleted items','archive','favorites','conversation history','new mail'. Without this, json_caret_is_page_scrollback may need >=2 generic hits ('inbox' is already there; add one Outlook-specific marker so the reroute fires).
2) Message-list detection — Outlook rows are div[role='option'] inside a role='listbox'/'Message list' (Gmail uses tr.zA / role='list'); the role-based pruner already drops role='list' nav and is_nav_chrome lists, but verify the listbox of options is classified nav (it should be: it is not a CONTENT_LIST and sits outside the focus path once the composer has focus). If it survives, add 'message list' to the nav name set.
3) Chrome lines — extend json_is_gmail_chrome_line (context.rs:816) or add an Outlook set: 'reply all','forward','archive','report','sweep','move to','flag','snooze','add a subject','discard'.
4) Sender attribution — lift the sender display-name from each message-group header (the text node adjacent to '<email@host>' + datetime) as the turn author and the reply target; 'You'/Sent-Items => author 'You'.

RECIPE / SELECTOR caveats to VERIFY LIVE (no login yet):
- composeSelector: aria-label is expected to be 'Message body' (Outlook's documented inline-reply body label); the fallback div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox'] covers a relabel. CONFIRM the exact aria-label casing/text on a real capture and pin COMPOSE['outlook'] + the ensureExpr assertion to it.
- Message-list row selector div[role='option'] (and the listbox/'Message list' container), the Reply button (button[aria-label='Reply'] vs a text-only 'Reply'), and the reading-pane body container (div.allowTextSelection / role='document') are best-guess from OWA structure — verify and tighten the waitFor selectors against artifacts/context-cdp/outlook/rawSnapshot.json once captured.
- Window title slice for resolve-hwnd.ps1: confirm whether it is 'Mail - <name> - Outlook' (personal) or 'Inbox | Microsoft 365' / 'Outlook' (work) and that the unread-count strip + |<>\"` cut yields a clean substring.

HARNESS WIRING (tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs): add an APPS['outlook'] entry { label:'gmail', url:'https://outlook.live.com/mail/0/inbox', titleHint:'Outlook', focus: <the async-IIFE built from recipeSteps using FOCUS_HELPERS waitFor/clickFirst/focusEl> } and a COMPOSE['outlook'] = the composeSelector. Mirror the Gmail focus IIFE shape (open row -> click Reply -> poll Message body -> click+focusEl) keeping the cumulative waitFor budget well under the 30s CDP evaluate timeout. Login already wired in tools/chrome-cdp-show-login.mjs:20.

PRIVACY: the dropped message-list rows can carry live Microsoft single-use codes; the structural role-prune (not the 85% linear strip_list_scrollback cut, which is Gmail-tuned and would leave 2-3 Outlook rows) is what removes them — confirm no 'verification code'/'single-use code' survives in the captured `screen` before declaring the surface clean.

specs.label: claude
recipeSteps: Open https://chatgpt.com/ in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true), then Page.enable + Runtime.enable + Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs / UIA tab-select do NOT build it). Settle ~2.5s for the Next.js SPA + auth hydration.

ENSURE LOGGED IN: if location.pathname starts with /auth or a 'Log in'/'Sign up' control is the only thing rendered (no main composer), surface a focusMiss ('not-logged-in') and stop — the dedicated capture browser must be pre-authenticated (mirrors the discord/whatsapp login gate). The logged-in shell is signalled by the composer #prompt-textarea OR the conversation-list nav existing in the DOM.

OPEN A CONVERSATION WITH HISTORY (do NOT capture the empty new-chat landing — it has zero turns): POLL the left sidebar history list for a real prior thread: waitFor("nav a[href^='/c/']", 12000). Click the FIRST such anchor (its href is /c/<uuid>) to load an existing conversation. Skip the 'New chat' button and any project/GPT-store nav. If no /c/ anchor exists (history hidden or brand-new account), fall back to the current /c/ URL only if turns are already present; otherwise return focusMiss ('no-conversation-with-history').

WAIT FOR RENDERED TURNS (poll, not fixed-sleep): waitFor("[data-message-author-role]", 12000), then POLL until document.querySelectorAll("[data-message-author-role]").length >= 2 so at least one user+assistant exchange is mounted (ChatGPT virtualizes long threads, but the most-recent turns nearest the composer are always rendered — no scroll needed for 'reply to this' / 'continue'). The conversation turns live under main article[data-testid^='conversation-turn-'] wrappers, each carrying a child [data-message-author-role].

SEAT THE CARET IN THE COMPOSER (poll): box = waitFor('#prompt-textarea', 12000) || waitFor("main form div[contenteditable='true']", 4000); then focusEl(box) — click + el.focus() + collapse a Range to the end (the composer is a ProseMirror contenteditable div, NOT a native textarea, so a bare .focus() loses to re-render; the click seats the caret).

RE-SEAT FOCUS RIGHT BEFORE THE UIA READ via ensureExpr('#prompt-textarea') (the harness's 4-attempt click+focus+caret-to-end loop) because the streaming UI / send-button enable re-renders the composer and can steal focus back to BODY. Verify document.activeElement.matches the composeSelector (its UIA elementName surfaces as 'Ask anything' / the placeholder, which looks_like_composer_field matches via 'ask'/'message').

Page.bringToFront once more, resolve the Chrome window HWND by document.title (title is like '<conversation title> - ChatGPT - Google Chrome' or 'ChatGPT - Google Chrome'; sanitize: strip any leading unread count, cut at the first |<>\"` char, slice to ~30 chars), then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> AND --selection --hwnd <hwnd>. Feed --tree stdout to context_prompt_smoke --label claude --require-prompt-json. ChatGPT's composer caret-context is thin/empty, so json_focused_field_is_rich is false and the formatter takes the fallback-tree branch (prune_ax_html_for_llm) — the per-author conversation lands in the 'screen' JSON key, the draft (if any) in fieldText.
specs.turnAttribution: ai-chat: ChatGPT exposes turn roles DETERMINISTICALLY via the DOM attribute data-message-author-role on each message node (value 'user' or 'assistant'; 'system'/'tool' rare and should be dropped). Each turn is wrapped in main article[data-testid^='conversation-turn-N'] and the message body lives under [data-message-author-role][data-message-id] (a markdown-rendered .markdown / div for assistant turns, a plain whitespace-pre-wrap div for user turns). Because role lives in an ATTRIBUTE (not visible text), the UIA --tree axHtml will NOT carry a literal 'User:'/'Assistant:' prefix — UIA only sees the rendered text + ARIA. So the extractor attributes POSITIONALLY/structurally like X (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns over the conversation landmark): each article[data-testid=conversation-turn-*] becomes one turn; the role attribute (user vs assistant) determines the author label. Render USER turns as 'You: <text>' (Discord/ChatGPT self convention — the speaker-prefix regex JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE already accepts 'You:') and ASSISTANT turns as 'ChatGPT: <text>' (the app name, analogous to Discord display-name / X handle attribution). Map the role to the author token at the per-turn-node boundary; never infer the speaker from a colon inside the body. participants[] = ['You','ChatGPT']. Order is document order = chronological. The user's current draft in the composer is NOT a turn (it goes to draft/fieldText, never to conversation[]).
specs.targetJsonExample:
{"app":"chrome.exe","url":"https://chatgpt.com/c/6a1f…","window":"Rust regex performance - ChatGPT","field":"Ask anything","surface":"ai-chat","participants":["You","ChatGPT"],"conversation":[{"author":"You","text":"How do I make this Rust regex faster? It's compiled inside a hot loop."},{"author":"ChatGPT","text":"Compile the Regex once with once_cell::Lazy instead of inside the loop — Regex::new is expensive and allocates a new DFA each call. Hoist it to a static and reuse it."},{"author":"You","text":"What if the pattern depends on a runtime variable?"},{"author":"ChatGPT","text":"Use a small LRU cache keyed by the pattern string, or build it once per distinct input outside the loop. If the variable part is just a literal, prefer regex::escape + a fixed shell rather than recompiling."}],"draft":"","screen":"You: How do I make this Rust regex faster? It's compiled inside a hot loop.
ChatGPT: Compile the Regex once with once_cell::Lazy instead of inside the loop — Regex::new is expensive and allocates a new DFA each call. Hoist it to a static and reuse it.
You: What if the pattern depends on a runtime variable?
ChatGPT: Use a small LRU cache keyed by the pattern string, or build it once per distinct input outside the loop. If the variable part is just a literal, prefer regex::escape + a fixed shell rather than recompiling."}
falseSpeakerRisks: Left sidebar conversation/history list: prior chat titles ('Rust regex performance', 'Trip to Jeddah', 'Untitled'), date-group dividers ('Today','Yesterday','Previous 7 Days','Previous 30 Days'), 'New chat', search, and the Projects/GPTs/Library/Sora nav links — all navigation, dropped by JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE (chats?/conversations?/recents?/recent threads?/sidebar/projects?) UNLESS they contain the focus

Top bar model picker: 'ChatGPT 5.2', 'ChatGPT', the model-selector dropdown button + its menu items ('GPT-5.2 Thinking','Auto','Get Plus'), 'Share' and 'Temporary chat' toggles — role=button/menu/menuitem, dropped by json_drop_subtree_role. CAUTION: 'ChatGPT' as a bare standalone line from the model picker must NOT be mistaken for an assistant author header; the author label comes from the data-message-author-role node boundary, not a free-floating 'ChatGPT' text node

Footer disclaimer: 'ChatGPT can make mistakes. Check important info.' and 'See Cookie Preferences.' — must be stripped (add to the low-signal / chrome line set; it co-occurs under the composer on every page)

Composer chrome: the 'Ask anything' placeholder echoed as content, '+' / attach / tools / dictate / send buttons, the slash-command and '@' mention popovers, 'Search'/'Reason'/'Deep research' tool chips — keep ONLY the contenteditable draft text

Per-message hover action row: 'Copy','Edit','Read aloud','Good response','Bad response','Switch model','Regenerate','Branch in new chat','Share' and the '< 2/3 >' response-variant pager — role=button, dropped; the bare pager counts ('2/3') would otherwise read as engagement noise
Code-block chrome inside assistant turns: language label tabs and the 'Copy code' / 'Edit' buttons that sit above fenced code (role=button) — keep the code text, drop the buttons
Streaming/stop affordances: 'Stop generating','Continue generating', the typing/thinking indicator ('Thinking…','Reasoned for 4s') — JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE / low-signal
Upsell + onboarding modals: 'Upgrade to Plus','Get Plus','Try advanced voice', cookie banner 'Accept all'/'Reject non-essential', and any 'You've reached your limit' notice
Browser/Chrome chrome from the axHtml root: 'Address and search bar' omnibox (json_is_omnibox), Minimize/Maximize/Close/Back/Forward/Reload, the Bookmarks toolbar, tab strip + other tabs' titles
Object-replacement / icon-font glyphs (￼, bullets, private-use codepoints) stripped by JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE/denoise_for_llm

OTP/verification-code or login/skeleton text if an auth page leaks before the conversation loads (defense-in-depth — already covered by the smoke gate's otp_or_login_code_noise_detected / login_or_skeleton_page_detected; must never reach the conversation text)
specs.notes:
DESIGNED FROM APP STRUCTURE + EXISTING PATTERNS — NOT LIVE-VERIFIED (the dedicated capture browser is not yet logged into ChatGPT). Everything below the load-bearing data-message-author-role contract needs a live capture pass (run: node tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs chatgpt after adding APPS.chatgpt + COMPOSE.chatgpt to tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs).

LABEL CHOICE: map onto the existing 'claude' gmail-style family (context_prompt_smoke.rs: min_context_chars('claude')=180, min_speaker_lines('claude')=1, is_chat_like_label includes 'claude'). ChatGPT is structurally the SAME surface as the already-specced Claude ai-chat (composer + role-attributed turns), so it rides the same gate thresholds with no new label needed. min_speaker_lines=1 means even a single attributed turn satisfies multiSpeakerContext, but a real history thread yields 2+ (You + ChatGPT) cleanly.

DETERMINISTIC ATTRIBUTION IS THE KEY ADVANTAGE: unlike X (positional @handle guessing) or Gmail (per-message-header sender), ChatGPT marks every turn with data-message-author-role='user'|'assistant'. This is the cleanest ai-chat surface in the spec set. BUT UIA exposes the RENDERED text, not the attribute — so the extractor must map role→author at the article[data-testid^='conversation-turn-'] node boundary in the ax tree, emitting 'You: …' (user) and 'ChatGPT: …' (assistant) so json_reconstruct_speaker_turns / JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE pick them up as flat 'Author: text' lines. If the live ax tree does NOT preserve a per-turn group boundary (i.e. user+assistant text collapses into one undelimited blob), an X-style PER-APP reconstructor keyed on the conversation-turn landmark would be needed — VERIFY this against a live rawSnapshot.json before assuming the GLOBAL speaker-turn path suffices.

SELECTORS TO VERIFY LIVE (ChatGPT ships frequent DOM refactors): (1) composer id #prompt-textarea is stable and long-lived but confirm it is still the contenteditable (fallback 'main form div[contenteditable=true]' included). (2) data-message-author-role and article[data-testid^='conversation-turn-'] are the documented turn markers — confirm both still present. (3) sidebar history anchors 'nav a[href^=\"/c/\"]' — the /c/<uuid> conversation route is stable; confirm the sidebar nav still uses plain anchors (vs a virtualized list). (4) the footer disclaimer 'ChatGPT can make mistakes' string — confirm exact wording for the chrome-line filter.

CAPTURE MODE: ContextMode::Tree (axHtml → prune_ax_html_for_llm), same as Discord — the composer caret-context is thin so the conversation lands in the 'screen' key, draft in fieldText. The flat 'screen' string in targetJsonExample is the back-compat shape context_prompt_smoke.rs and the current extractor consume; conversation[]/participants[]/draft are the additive ideal structured shape (no new JSON keys are emitted by context.rs today — the role/author info is encoded as 'Author: text' lines per the flat output contract in src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs).

KEY FILES (absolute): E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs (add APPS.chatgpt recipe + COMPOSE.chatgpt selector, mirror the discord/claude pattern); E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (format_context_for_prompt_json, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns @ ~976, JSON_NAV_NAME_RE @603, JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE @647, json_is_omnibox @1030 — add 'chatgpt can make mistakes' + 'ask anything' placeholder to the chrome/low-signal sets); E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs (gate: looks_like_composer_field @863 already matches 'ask'/'message'/'prompt', is_chat_like_label @849 / min_context_chars @831 include 'claude'). NO examples/ tree exists (per project memory the electron clone was deleted) — context.rs is the source of truth.

specs.label: gmail
recipeSteps: Open https://gemini.google.com/app in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true), then Page.enable + Runtime.enable + Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs / UIA tab-select do NOT build it). Settle ~2.5s for the Angular SPA shell to boot.

ENSURE LOGGED IN: if location redirects to accounts.google.com / a 'Sign in' button is present and no conversation shell renders, the run cannot proceed - surface a focusMiss ('not-logged-in') and stop. The dedicated capture browser must be pre-authenticated with the Google account (NOT logged in yet at spec time - mark for live verification).

OPEN A CONVERSATION WITH VISIBLE HISTORY (poll, never fixed-sleep): the recents live in the left side nav. POLL up to ~12s for a recent-conversation entry - waitFor('side-nav-action-button[data-test-id="conversation"], [data-test-id="conversation"], .conversation-items-container .conversation, a[href*="/app/"]') and click the FIRST one whose href/id is a real conversation (a hex/uuid path segment under /app/, NOT the 'New chat' / 'Explore Gems' / 'Settings & help' nav rows). If the side nav is collapsed, first click the hamburger ('[data-test-id="side-nav-menu-button"], button[aria-label*="Main menu" i], button[aria-label*="Expand" i]') then re-poll for the recents list.

WAIT FOR THE THREAD TO RENDER (poll, don't sleep): waitFor('user-query, model-response, message-content, .conversation-container') so the turn stream is mounted. POLL until at least one <user-query> AND one <model-response> custom element exist (document.querySelectorAll('user-query').length>=1 && document.querySelectorAll('model-response').length>=1) so the capture has real back-and-forth depth - Gemini renders turns lazily and streams the last answer.

DISMISS / IGNORE blocking overlays if present (poll up to ~4s): close any onboarding 'Meet Gemini' dialog, cookie consent, or 'Gemini Apps Activity' bottom sheet by clicking its Close / 'No thanks' / 'Got it' control (scan div[role="dialog"] [aria-label="Close" i], button matching /no thanks|got it|dismiss|not now/i). Do not block the recipe on this - proceed once the turn stream is visible.

SEAT THE CARET IN THE COMPOSER (poll): box = waitFor('rich-textarea div.ql-editor[contenteditable="true"]') || waitFor('div[contenteditable="true"][role="textbox"]'); the Gemini input is a Quill rich-text editor (div.ql-editor) inside the <rich-textarea> component. focusEl(box): el.scrollIntoView({block:'center'}); el.click(); el.focus(); collapse a Range to end (selectNodeContents + collapse(false)).

RE-SEAT focus immediately before the UIA read via ensureExpr('div.ql-editor[contenteditable="true"], rich-textarea div[contenteditable="true"][role="textbox"]') - the 4-attempt click+focus+caret-to-end loop - because Angular re-renders / the streamed answer finishing can steal focus back to the document. Verify document.activeElement.matches the selector (NOT BODY, NOT the omnibox) before reading.

Page.bringToFront once more, resolve the Chrome window HWND by document.title slice (occlusion-proof; title is like '<first words of the conversation> - Gemini - Google Chrome', sanitized: cut at the first |<>\"` char, slice to ~30 chars; fall back to titleHint 'Gemini' when the SPA title is bare). Gate the native read on a DECIMAL hwnd; if unresolved, emit the empty failure snapshot (no foreground fallback).

CAPTURE with Tree mode: run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> and --selection --hwnd <hwnd>, then feed --tree stdout to context_prompt_smoke --label gmail --require-prompt-json. The composer caret-context is thin/empty at dictation time, so the axHtml tree (NOT beforeCaret) is the load-bearing capture; the per-turn user/assistant attribution is reconstructed by prune_ax_html_for_llm / json_reconstruct_speaker_turns over the <user-query>/<model-response> groups.
specs.turnAttribution: ai-chat: USER vs ASSISTANT (app-name = Gemini). Gemini's web DOM exposes each turn as a custom element - <user-query> wraps the human prompt (the typed text lives in a child like .query-text / [data-test-id=\"user-query-content\"]) and <model-response> wraps Gemini's answer (the rendered markdown lives in .markdown / message-content / [data-test-id=\"model-response-text\"]). Unlike ChatGPT (data-message-author-role=user|assistant) or Claude (user-message vs assistant blocks), Gemini has NO role attribute - the TAG NAME itself is the role marker: user-query => USER turn, model-response => ASSISTANT turn. In the UIA --tree these surface as sibling group/article nodes under the conversation container; prune_ax_html_for_llm / json_reconstruct_speaker_turns walk them in document order. The extractor must render each as a flat 'Author: text' line per the context.rs contract (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns emits format!(\"{author}: {message}\"), counted by JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE ^\\s*(?:@?[name]{2,40}|You|Me):\\s+\\S): the user's own turns => 'You:' (the self label the gate already special-cases) and Gemini's turns => 'Gemini:' (a valid speaker label under the regex). So a turn pair becomes 'You: <prompt>' then 'Gemini: <answer>'. The current user's in-progress draft (the composer's ql-editor text, empty at dictation start) stays separate as draft/fieldText - it must NOT be emitted as a 'You:' conversation turn.
specs.targetJsonExample: {"app":"chrome.exe","url":"https://gemini.google.com/app/8f3c1a92b7d04e6f","window":"Best way to deploy a Tauri app - Gemini","field":"Enter a prompt here","surface":"ai-chat","participants":["You","Gemini"],"conversation":[{"author":"You","text":"What's the best way to code-sign and notarize a Tauri app for Windows?"},{"author":"Gemini","text":"For Windows you'll want an Authenticode certificate (EV or OV) and sign the bundled .exe/.msi with signtool during your tauri build. The cleanest path is to wire signing into tauri.conf.json's bundle.windows.certificateThumbprint and run the build on a machine where the cert is installed."},{"author":"You","text":"Can I do that on a GitHub Actions runner instead of locally?"},{"author":"Gemini","text":"Yes. Store the cert as a base64 secret, import it into the runner's cert store at job start, then point signtool at its thumbprint. For EV certs that require a hardware token you'll instead need a self-hosted runner or a cloud signing service like Azure Trusted Signing."}],"draft":"and how do I handle the SmartScreen reputation warning"}
falseSpeakerRisks: Footer disclaimer: 'Gemini can make mistakes, so double-check it' / 'Gemini can make mistakes. Check important info' - a persistent banner under the composer; must be filtered (it is NOT a turn). Risk that a naive parser reads it as a 'Gemini:' speaker.

Left side-nav recents roster: the 'Recent' conversation list (side-nav-action-button / .conversation-items-container entries showing prior chat titles), 'New chat', 'Explore Gems', 'Gems', 'Settings & help', 'Activity', 'Upgrade' / 'Get the app' - all navigation, dropped by JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE (recents/sidebar/navigation). Each recent title could be mis-read as a turn.

Suggestion / prompt-starter chips: the rounded suggestion buttons shown on a fresh chat or under an answer ('Help me write', 'Brainstorm', 'Surprise me', follow-up suggestion chips). role=button => dropped by json_drop_subtree_role; must not become conversation text.

Model/version selector + headers: the 'Gemini' / 'Gemini Advanced' / '2.5 Pro' / '2.5 Flash' model-name dropdown at the top, and any 'PRO' / 'Thinking' / 'with research' badges - top-bar chrome, not a speaker.

Per-answer action toolbar: 'Show drafts', 'Regenerate', the thumbs up/down, 'Share & export', 'Copy', 'Modify response', 'Sources' / citation chips, 'Double-check response with Google' - role=button/toolbar chrome under each model-response; drop.
Composer placeholder echoed as content: 'Enter a prompt here' / 'Ask Gemini' (the empty ql-editor placeholder) - the field name, not a turn.
Top-bar account / app chrome: 'Gemini Apps Activity', the Google account avatar + email, 'Main menu' hamburger, 'Open Gemini in a new tab', the 'New chat' pencil button.

Chrome/OS chrome (covered by GLOBAL rules): 'Address and search bar' omnibox (gemini.google.com/...), Minimize/Maximize/Close/Back/Forward/Reload, the Bookmarks toolbar, tab strip - drop via json_is_omnibox / json_drop_subtree_role.

Streaming/loading artifacts: a partially-streamed model-response, 'Generating...', skeleton shimmer, or the stop-generating square - poll until the turn stream is stable so a half-rendered answer isn't captured.
OTP/login-code or account-recovery text (defense-in-depth, covered by the smoke gate's otp_or_login_code_noise check) - not expected inside a chat but must never leak.
specs.notes: DESIGNED FROM APP STRUCTURE + EXISTING PATTERNS - NOT LIVE-VERIFIED (app is not logged in yet). LIVE-VERIFY before trusting: (1) the side-nav recents selector to open a conversation WITH history - candidates 'side-nav-action-button[data-test-id=\"conversation\"]', '.conversation-items-container .conversation', 'a[href*=\"/app/\"]'; (2) the composer selector - Gemini's input is a Quill editor 'rich-textarea div.ql-editor[contenteditable=\"true\"]' (also matches div[contenteditable=\"true\"][role=\"textbox\"]); confirm the composer aria-label/elementName ('Enter a prompt here' vs 'Ask Gemini') for the looks_like_composer_field gate and the COMPOSE map; (3) that <user-query>/<model-response> custom-element tags survive into the UIA axHtml as distinguishable group nodes (Angular custom elements usually become role=group/region/article in the a11y tree - the role-based pruner keys on the GROUP structure + leading text, not the literal tag, so verify json_reconstruct_speaker_turns emits 'You:'/'Gemini:' over them). CONTRACT MAPPING: this rides the GLOBAL chat-thread path identical to Discord/Messenger - no Gemini-specific Rust branch is required if the tree reconstructs cleanly; the extractor emits the existing app/url/window/field/screen keys with the thread as 'You: ...' / 'Gemini: ...' lines (the conversation[]/participants[]/draft view in targetJsonExample is the logical shape those flat lines encode). PER-APP TUNING POINTS to confirm against a live capture: add 'gemini' / 'recent' to JSON_NAV_NAME_RE if the recents list isn't already dropped (it carries 'recents?' which should match), and ensure the 'Gemini can make mistakes' footer line is filtered (extend JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE or a per-app chrome-line set if it survives). label 'gmail' is used (NOT a dedicated 'gemini' label) only as the closest existing analyzer family for the smoke gate's --label arg; the user's stated 'gmail-style family' label maps here - re-evaluate once a real capture exists since ai-chat depth thresholds may differ from the email-thread family. KEY FILES (absolute): E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (format_context_for_prompt_json @2001, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns @1534, JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE @615, JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE @609, JSON_NAV_NAME_RE @603, JSON_FALSE_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE @704, prune_ax_html_for_llm @1775); harness to add the APPS['gemini'] recipe + COMPOSE['gemini'] selector: E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs; quality gate: E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs; per-app spec style mirrored from E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/docs/plans/CONTEXT_JSON_SPECS.json.

specs.label: claude
recipeSteps: Open https://claude.ai/recents in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chromium builds the web a11y tree the UIA --tree read depends on (background tabs / UIA tab-select do NOT build it). After Page.bringToFront, resolvePageContext on host 'claude.ai' (the about:blank-zombie / SW-hijack guard from the harness) so every evaluate threads the real-origin contextId.

ENSURE LOGGED IN: if location.pathname starts with /login or a 'Log in' / 'Sign in' control is present and no conversation list renders, surface a focusMiss ('not-logged-in') and stop — the dedicated capture browser must be pre-authenticated. Do NOT read a login/onboarding page as a usable capture.

OPEN A CONVERSATION WITH HISTORY (do not capture the empty /new composer — it has no turns). POLL up to ~12s for the recents list, then click the first real past conversation: waitFor a link with href^='/chat/' (or '/recents' list rows: a[href*="/chat/"]), prefer one whose visible title is NOT 'New chat'. Click it to navigate to https://claude.ai/chat/<uuid>. (Alternative entry: navigating straight to a known /chat/<uuid> URL also works if one is on hand.)

POLL for the rendered transcript BEFORE focusing: waitFor up to ~15s for at least one user turn AND one assistant turn to exist — [data-testid="user-message"] present AND .font-claude-message present (or a [data-message-author-role] / assistant block). Require >=1 of each so attribution has both roles; never fixed-sleep, the SPA hydrates the message log after route change.

POLL for the composer to mount and SEAT THE CARET: waitFor up to ~12s for div[contenteditable="true"].ProseMirror (aria 'Write your prompt to Claude'); scrollIntoView, click it, then focusEl (el.focus() + collapse a Range to end) so document.activeElement is the ProseMirror editor.

RE-SEAT + VERIFY focus right before the UIA read via the ensureExpr retry loop on the composeSelector (click + el.focus() + caret-to-end, up to 4x at ~400ms): assert document.activeElement.matches the ProseMirror composer — the SPA re-renders the input and silently drops focus, and a focus-miss makes winstt_context.exe read the window title instead of the field. Re-issue Page.bringToFront.

Resolve the Chrome window HWND by document.title (sanitize: strip a leading unread count, cut at the first |<>"` char, slice ~30 chars — Claude titles read like 'Conversation title - Claude' or 'Claude'); confirm a DECIMAL hwnd before reading. Then run the SAME native helper dictation uses: winstt_context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> AND winstt_context.exe --selection --hwnd <hwnd>. The composer is empty at dictation start so --selection is normally blank; the conversation signal is in --tree's axHtml (pruned to the 'screen' section).
specs.turnAttribution: AI-chat, two roles: USER vs ASSISTANT (app name 'Claude'). In the live DOM, a user turn is the [data-testid=\"user-message\"] block (Claude does NOT expose data-message-author-role like ChatGPT — its markers are the user-message testid and the assistant content class) and an assistant turn is the .font-claude-message block (the streamed-response container). The extractor does NOT read the DOM — it reads the Chrome UIA --tree, where each rendered turn surfaces as an <item>/<group>/<row> node whose value already arrives joined as 'User: <text>' / 'Claude: <text>'-shaped lines (matching the existing codex/ChatGPT path). The Rust formatter (prune_ax_html_for_llm -> json_resolve_landmark -> json_reconstruct_speaker_turns in src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs) scopes to the Conversation content landmark on the composer's focus path, drops the recents/projects nav lists, and emits flat 'Author: text' lines into the 'screen' key. Render the user's own prompts as 'User:' (Claude's a11y tree labels the human turns generically; emit author 'User') and Claude's replies as 'Claude:'. No new JSON keys — multi-author is encoded purely as 'Author: text' lines, one turn per line, in document order, with the empty composer carrying no turn. If the captured UIA value is the bare role text without a name attribute, attribute positionally: a [data-testid=user-message] subtree => User, a .font-claude-message subtree => Claude.
specs.targetJsonExample:
{
  "app": "chrome.exe",
  "url": "https://claude.ai/chat/8f2c1a04-...",
  "window": "Refactor the context parser - Claude",
  "field": "Write your prompt to Claude",
  "screen": "User: Can you summarize the error report from the context sidecar?\nClaude: The failing component is winstt_context.exe — the UIA --tree read returns an empty snapshot when the HWND can't be resolved, so the formatter falls back to the foreground window.\nUser: Draft the follow-up message describing the workaround.\nClaude: Here's a draft you can send: 'We worked around it by emitting an explicit empty-snapshot on hwnd-unresolved instead of a foreground fallback, which stops the false replyReady=true verdicts.'",
  "fieldText": ""
}
falseSpeakerRisks: LEFT CHAT-HISTORY SIDEBAR (the headline leak): the recents/history list of PAST conversation titles (e.g. 'Refactor the context parser', 'Trip planning to Jeddah', 'Untitled') — these are nav rows, NOT turns; dropped as a <list name="Recents"/"Starred"/"Projects"/"Chats"> via JSON_NAV_NAME_RE (covers recents?/projects?/chats?/threads?). They must never become 'Author: text' lines.
Sidebar nav items: 'New chat', 'Chats', 'Projects', 'Artifacts', 'Recents', 'Starred', search box ('Search your chats'), and the collapse/expand rail toggle.

Model / chrome pills around the composer: model selector ('Claude Sonnet 4.5' / 'Opus 4.x' / 'Use style' / 'Research' / 'Extended thinking' / 'Web search'), the attach/upload + send buttons, '+' / tools menu — these are role=button/menu/combo subtrees dropped by json_drop_subtree_role.
Composer placeholder echoed as content: 'Write your prompt to Claude' / 'How can I help you today?' / 'Reply to Claude' — the empty-composer placeholder, not a user turn.
Per-message action affordances: 'Copy', 'Retry', 'Edit', 'Good response' / 'Bad response' (thumbs), 'Continue', 'Share', the message-options menu — low-signal UI, not body text.

Bottom disclaimer / usage footers: 'Claude can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.', 'Claude is generating...', usage/limit banners ('You are approaching your usage limit', 'Resets at ...', 'Upgrade to Max'), 'Daily limit' notices.
Top-bar / account chrome: the conversation-title heading echoed in the doc (keep only as window/threadSubject, not a turn), the account/avatar menu, project name breadcrumb, 'Share' button.
Artifact / canvas side panel chrome if open (code/preview tabs, 'Copy'/'Download'/'Publish') — canvas content is not exposed cleanly via UIA and is not a conversation turn.

Browser/OS chrome already covered by GLOBAL rules: 'Address and search bar' omnibox (json_is_omnibox), Back/Forward/Reload, tab strip, Bookmarks toolbar, window controls — including OTHER tabs' titles (e.g. a Gmail/ChatGPT tab) which must not be read as Claude turns.
specs.notes:
DESIGN-ONLY (app not logged in; browser was NOT driven) — items marked NEEDS-LIVE-VERIFY below.

ALREADY WIRED IN-REPO: the 'claude' integration is largely pre-built. (1) Label family: context_prompt_smoke.rs:445 maps app contains 'claude' -> label 'claude' (gmail-style/AI-chat family, sibling of codex). (2) URL inference: infer_fixture_url + extract_known_url already special-case claude.ai (-> https://claude.ai/chat/abc-123). (3) A PASSING unit test exists: context.rs claude_chat_keeps_dialog_and_drops_project_sidebar — window 'Claude', field 'Message Claude', url claude.ai/chat/123, ax with <list name=\"Projects\"> (dropped) + <group name=\"Conversation\"> of <item name=\"User: ...\"/>/<item name=\"Claude: ...\"/> kept into 'screen'. So the EXTRACTOR ALREADY HANDLES the AI-chat shape; this spec is the CAPTURE harness recipe + verdict spec, not new Rust.

OUTPUT CONTRACT: AI-chat content lands in the 'screen' key (the prune_ax_html_for_llm output), as flat 'Author: text' lines — NOT beforeCaret. Claude's empty ProseMirror composer yields a thin focused field, so json_focused_field_is_rich is false and the formatter takes the fallback-tree branch (json_build_fallback_tree_section -> prune_ax_html_for_llm). That resolves the Conversation landmark on the composer's focus path and reconstructs speaker turns. No new JSON keys are introduced (the user's no-new-keys requirement is satisfied: roles are encoded as 'User:'/'Claude:' line prefixes inside 'screen').

HARNESS INTEGRATION: add to tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs an APPS['claude'] entry (label:'claude', url:'https://claude.ai/recents', titleHint:'Claude', focus: the recipeSteps IIFE) and a COMPOSE['claude'] = the composeSelector. Reuse FOCUS_HELPERS (waitFor/clickFirst/focusEl) and ensureExpr exactly like the existing apps. Note the existing app titleHint for X is ' / X'; for Claude use 'Claude'.

NEEDS-LIVE-VERIFY (could not drive the browser): (a) the EXACT recents-list selector — assumed a[href*=\"/chat/\"]; confirm vs the actual sidebar/recents markup. (b) the composer aria-label exact text — the codebase test uses 'Message Claude' (element_name) but the current product placeholder is 'Write your prompt to Claude'; the composeSelector matches BOTH via aria-label substring + .ProseMirror, but confirm which winstt_context.exe reports as elementName (that becomes 'field'). (c) WHETHER Claude's UIA tree exposes each turn as a single 'User: text' / 'Claude: text' <item> (the assumed/tested shape) OR as SPLIT author/body child nodes like Discord — if split, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns still attributes via the item's name; if the human turn surfaces with NO author label, the positional fallback ([data-testid=user-message]=>User, .font-claude-message=>Claude) is a DOM-side hint that the UIA reader does not see, so confirm the native tree carries a usable author token, else the assistant-only attribution may need a per-app rule. (d) the window title format (assumed '<title> - Claude'); confirm before trusting HWND-by-title. (e) the recents page may itself need a click to fully render the chat list (lazy-load) — POLL, don't sleep.

REFERENCE: existing live captures + recipes for the sibling AI-chat surface (codex/ChatGPT) and the chat reconstructor live at src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (prune_ax_html_for_llm @1775, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns @1534, json_resolve_landmark @1729, JSON_NAV_NAME_RE @603, JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE @615), the quality gate at src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs (fixture_label @429, fixture_prompt_reply_ready @515), and the harness pattern at tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs (APPS map, COMPOSE map, ensureExpr @327, resolvePageContext @444).
